description: Master index of all available data sources. AI agent reads this file first to discover what data exists and how to fetch it.

agent_usage.step_1: Read this index.json to discover available sources
agent_usage.step_2: Read the specific source recipe (e.g. catalog/imf.json) for full endpoint details
agent_usage.step_3: Call agent.py with the right parameters
agent_usage.step_4: Get back CSV and/or JSON output

sources.name: Wikipedia Pageviews API
sources.description: Daily pageview statistics for ANY Wikipedia article. Track public interest in any topic over time.
what_you_can_get: Daily pageviews for any Wikipedia article
Top 1000 most viewed articles per day
example_questions: How much public interest is there in 'Mortgage rates' over time?
Which economic topics are trending on Wikipedia this week?

sources.name: IMF Data (International Monetary Fund)
sources.description: Global economic and financial statistics across 190+ countries. GDP, inflation, unemployment, debt, current account, and more.
what_you_can_get: GDP growth rate for any country
example_questions: What is the GDP growth trajectory of emerging markets since 2000?
Which countries have the highest debt-to-GDP ratios?
How does US unemployment compare to EU countries historically?

sources.name: World Bank Indicators API
sources.description: Global development indicators across 200+ countries. Broader than IMF — covers education, health, environment, infrastructure, and more.
example_questions: How has CO2 emissions per capita changed by country since 1990?
What is the correlation between education spending and GDP growth?
Which countries have the highest FDI inflows relative to GDP?

sources.name: arXiv API
sources.description: Open access research papers across AI, economics, finance, physics, math, and more. Track publication cadence, emerging topics, and research trends.
what_you_can_get: Publication count by category per month/year
Word count from titles and abstracts
Any arXiv category (CS, Economics, Finance, Physics, Math, etc.)
example_questions: How many AI papers mention 'housing market' in the abstract?
What are the most published topics in quantitative finance this year?

sources.name: OFAC SDN List
sources.description: US Treasury Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons List. Complete sanctions list updated daily.
what_you_can_get: Full list of sanctioned individuals, entities, vessels, aircraft
Sanctions program (IRAN, RUSSIA, DPRK, SDGT, etc.)
example_questions: How many Russian entities have been added to SDN list since 2022?
Which sanctions programs have grown the most in the last year?
Are there any SDN entries linked to a specific country or sector?

imf_to_worldbank_mapping._note: IMF DataMapper currently blocks automated requests. When --source imf is unavailable, fall back to --source worldbank using the equivalent indicator code below.
